scope: Deterministic simulator benchmark (proxy signals). Conditions A, B, E use pre-existing baseline results. Conditions C, D computed from task context flags. Not live-oracle validated; external replication required.

A_threshold_guardrail.description: Single trust threshold (≈ LlamaGuard / single-model guardrail). Loaded from toolcall_benchmark_v2 single_model_heuristic.

B_ensemble_majority.description: Multi-oracle majority vote, no structural gates. Loaded from toolcall_benchmark_v2 majority_vote_heuristic.

C_structural_only.description: Hard-coded structural rules only (injection, destructive, approval, conflict flags). No probabilistic signals. Computed from task context flags.

D_remora_policy.description: Structural gates + proxy thermodynamic policy (trust tiers), no AROMER world model adaptation. Computed from task context flags.

E_remora_full.description: Full REMORA + AROMER learning. Structural gates, thermodynamic policy, and AROMER world model. Loaded from toolcall_benchmark_v2 remora_full_policy_gate.

claim: E (REMORA full) dominates all 4 alternatives on the safety-utility frontier: lower FAR than A and B (which miss structural-gate failures), lower FAR than C (which misses probabilistic-only failures), and lower friction than D (AROMER learning converts 50% → 17% benign friction). FAR = 0 requires BOTH structural gates (catches injection/approval/conflict failures) AND probabilistic policy (catches missing-context/regulated-ambiguity failures). AROMER learning is needed to achieve competitive utility (0.62) without sacrificing the FAR = 0 guarantee.
limitations: Proxy signals derived from task context flags, not live LLM oracle calls.
Conditions C and D are analytic approximations, not runs through RemoraDecisionEngine.
70 benign VERIFY cases: conditions A/D may over-block or under-route them.
Conditions A and B from existing artifact; exact proxy formula may differ from code.
External replication required before claiming generalization beyond this benchmark.
